title: Chapter 2555 - 38: Eccentric Corpse_3
content:
Chapter 2555: Chapter 38: Eccentric Corpse_3

But if it’s only related to an Elder from Yin Corpse Valley, using Causality Skill to calculate, there shouldn’t be much issue.

Moreover, he’s only calculating a bit, not deeply.

Just knowing what’s recorded on this page is enough.

Mo Hua used Divine Sense Control Ink again, laid down a Formation, and strengthened the defense of the guest room. Then, following the Great Wilderness Demon Bone Divination Skill, he performed a divination on the Mysterious Corpse Treasure Manual, focusing on the causality of the erased text on the third-to-last page.

The more specific and directed the causality divination, the more accurate, consuming less Divine Thought and carrying lower risk, with a reduced chance of misfortune.

Mo Hua has become increasingly familiar with the acupoints of divination.

Divine Thought was consumed, and the divination skill was activated.

Within the phosphorus flames, the causality reversed, and visions appeared.

The content originally erased by Blood Ink on the third page of the Mysterious Corpse Treasure Manual became lighter from being deep, then turned bright red, and gradually faded away.

The erased words began to reappear one by one...

"Little..."

Little?

Mo Hua was startled and then continued to watch, seeing causality reverse and the blood stains fade, revealing two more words:

"Flying Sky..."

Here, the causality began to stall, seemingly involving certain secrets, difficult to glimpse.

Mo Hua expended more Divine Thought.

The Great Wilderness Demon Bone Divination accelerated its operation, the phosphorus flames grew more intense, and cracks increased on the demon bone.

Finally, another word appeared:

"Spirit..."

Spirit?

Mo Hua was even more surprised that he discovered the word "Spirit" in a Demon Sect Corpse Path treasure manual?

Finally, unsurprisingly, it was a "Corpse" word.

At this point, the demon bone completely shattered, the phosphorus flames gradually extinguished.

Mo Hua’s Divine Thought was also consumed to the limit, so he stopped the causality divination, piecing together the words he divined in his mind:

"Little... Flying Sky... Spirit... Corpse..."

Mo Hua felt a chill in his heart.

Little Flying Sky Spirit Corpse?

What is this?

Not a Blood Corpse, not an Evil Corpse, not a Yin Corpse, but instead... a Spirit Corpse?

The inclusion of "Flying Sky" indicates it’s a type of "Flying Zombie"? Capable of soaring into the sky, possessing Feather Transformation abilities?

Why add "Little" then?

Mo Hua felt that the "Little Flying Sky Spirit Corpse" erased in Yin Corpse Valley’s Mysterious Corpse Treasure Manual was somewhat inexplicable and peculiar.

And there was an inexplicable sense of familiarity.

Mo Hua began to think, but after pondering for a long time, he suddenly realized that there were many fragmented blanks in his mind.

Past memories had faded considerably. Many people, many things, had become somewhat blurred in his mind.

Especially many details, he didn’t remember as clearly as before.

Mo Hua felt a slight chill inside.

This is the aftermath of the "fateful evilness Fierce Ghost" Life Chart arrangement.

Once one falls into it, either they lose their mind, becoming a Demon Head who kills indiscriminately.

Or they lose their memory, becoming a Puppet.

Then something bizarre would descend, controlled by the Uncle...

Mo Hua sighed, after pondering for a moment, he put away the Mysterious Corpse Treasure Manual.

Zombies, these things, understanding them a bit is enough, researching them too deeply has no use.

As a Righteous Cultivator, in this lifetime, he couldn’t possibly go into Corpse Refinement or raising corpses, it’s not worth expending much thought on this...

The pressing issue is still solving the Life Chart problem and the Core Formation.

For the Life Chart issue, he needs to find some "anchors" to preserve memory and stabilize his humanity.

As for Core Formation, he can only proceed to the Great Wilderness and act accordingly.

But the problem is, the Great Wilderness is currently engulfed in rebellious warfare.

Crossing over, he will inevitably face all sorts of fierce contests and killings.

In the turmoil, being alone grants convenience in actions, but it’s sure to lead to conflicts and disputes.

The most troublesome is that to avoid Life Chart backlash, he can’t casually strike kills.

In this case, customarily, it’s best to find a "guard" to travel with.

Thus, if the "Big Tiger" is still around, it could be a good "companion".

Additionally, there’s the issue of enhancing the Divine Sense.

To achieve Core Formation, the primary task is to find a way to elevate Divine Sense to the Second Grade, twenty-four patterns Realm.

And the battle with Elder Xu from Yin Corpse Valley further confirmed something for Mo Hua.

That is, the Divine Thought into sword with Second Grade, twenty-one patterns can indeed slay Golden Core Cultivators.

Furthermore, it’s not just simple "slaying", but instantaneous "instant kill".

Slaying and instant killing are not the same concept.

Mo Hua’s gaze was profound.

In other words, as long as his Divine Sense is strong enough, the Godslaying Sword can emerge and "instant kill" any cultivator whose Divine Sense Realm is lower than his.

His current Divine Sense Line is the "slaughter line".

It can ignore flesh, ignore Spiritual Power, and instantly slay any cultivator.

This is the true power of Divine Sense Proving the Dao.

Therefore, he must use all means to enhance the Divine Sense.

Not just for Core Formation, but also to raise the Divine Thought into sword "instant kill" slaughter line, so the upcoming dangerous journey in the Great Wilderness can be truly secure.

Although breaking the killing precept could infringe on the fateful evilness.

But the choice to kill or not must be within his own hands.

Moreover, as long as he learns stronger Causality Skills, able to restrain the "fateful evilness Fierce Ghost" arrangement set by the Uncle, or if one day he possesses the capability to truly contend against the Uncle, no longer being suppressed by the fateful evilness.

Then, the Taixu Divine Thought into sword can break through "limitations", truly emerge, and slay all around without hesitation.

Form into Dao, the Divine Thought damage ignores flesh defenses, instant kill slaying.

No matter the status, the strength of flesh, the amount of Spiritual Power, below the Divine Thought into sword’s slaughter line, all beings are equal.

Whoever he glances at must die.

This is the truly terrifying power of killing.

Mo Hua felt stirred, then calmed down a moment later, letting out a slight sigh.

"It’s just..."

Contending with the Uncle, is as hard as ascending to heaven...

Enhancing Divine Sense is also extremely difficult.

His current Divine Thought of twenty-one patterns is already stronger than many Golden Core Cultivators in the Initial Stage, improving each pattern thereafter is very difficult.

Reaching the Second Grade, twenty-four patterns is like crossing a Heavenly Chasm.

Mo Hua calculated with his fingers, silently performed a Calculation for a while, then looked toward the south, silently thinking:

"Hope during the Great Wilderness war, there will be the opportunity I seek..."

...

The next day, Mo Hua went out again to seek a boat permit.

However, after searching for half a day, he still found nothing.

Without exposing his identity, as an outsider Loose Cultivator with no ties, it’s nearly impossible to obtain a boat permit.

Even offering a high price, no one would sell it to him.

There’s nothing in this world that can’t be bought with Spirit Stone.

If there is, it’s because you don’t have enough Spirit Stone.

Mo Hua, now not what he used to be, had enough Spirit Stones on him, but he didn’t dare to actually spend too many Spirit Stones to buy a boat permit.

Firstly, it easily arouses suspicion.

Secondly, displaying wealth also easily attracts coveting.

He fears that before he even boards the ship, daring cultivators would want to kill him for his possessions.

Heaven has a good nature, Mo Hua truly doesn’t want to kill.

After wandering around, Mo Hua encountered obstacles everywhere and eventually had to temporarily give up.

But on the way back to the inn, he caught a glimpse of a notice posted on the street with the corner of his eye.

The notice stated a large fleet was recruiting "guards" for crossing the Sand Sea.

Mo Hua’s eyes brightened.

He didn’t know the exact requirements, duties, or dangers involved in being a "guard".

But considering he is the winner of the Qianxue Sword Discussion Conference, with Foundation Establishment Late Stage cultivation, proficient in various Five Elements Spells, surely he’s more than capable for this guard role...

With determination, Mo Hua followed the guidance on the notice and walked into a nearby boat firm...
